text:
When building a website, security is essential to protect both user data and the site itself. One of the first measures is using HTTPS, which encrypts data sent between the browser and server, preventing hackers from stealing personal information.

Another crucial step is authentication. Strong passwords, two-factor authentication, and secure login systems help ensure only authorized users can access sensitive areas.

Input validation is equally important. By checking and cleaning data users submit, you can block malicious code like SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS).

Common vulnerabilities include weak passwords, outdated software, and poor error handling. Regular updates and security testing keep these risks under control, making web development safer for everyone.
